content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 3, 1987


Walter White
11124 Excelsior Dr., #21
Norwalk, CA 90650

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-87-140

Dear Mr. White:

Enclosed is a copy of the letter I mailed to you in June in
response to your May letter. I cannot explain why you did not
receive it.

We have no record of your July 1 letter, nor do we have any
check from you. As I explained in my June letter, campaign
reports are not filed with us. You should contact the
Secretary of State's office to obtain copies of these reports.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh
Enclosure


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

F P P C

August 29, 1987
SEP 1 8 12 AM '87

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel
California Fair Political
    Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA.  95804-0807


Dear Ms. Griffiths,

     I had previously written in May to the Fair Political
Practices Commission inquiring as to whether a group known
by the name "CAUSA" (Confederation of Associations for the
Unity of Societies of the Americas) had filed a report as
a major donor for the period of 1983-1984.

     My inquiry weas ignored.

     I wrote to you on July 1, 1987. At that letter I
referred to my prior letter. I also requested information
as to whether the CAUSA group filed reports for the years
1985 and 1986.

     I also enclosed a signed check to pay for copies
of any filings made by CAUSA for the specified years.

     I have not received any response.

     CAUSA did make a $5,000. contribution to a Republican
party group. I have a copy of the Republican party group
listing the contribution.

     May I please obtain an answer to my inquiry?


Sincerely,

Walter White
Walter White
11124 Excelsior Dr., #21
Norwalk, California 90650

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 15, 1987


Walter E. White
11124 Excelsior Dr., #21
Norwalk, CA 90650

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 87-140

Dear Mr. White:

You have written seeking advice concerning the reporting
obligations of the Confederation of Associations for the Unity
of Societies of the Americas (CAUSA). Under the Political
Reform Act (the "Act"),1/ the Commission provides advice to
persons whose duties under the Act are in question. (Section
83114.) We do not provide advice to third parties about
another person's duties under the Act. (Regulation
18329(b)(8)(B).)

We do provide copies of documents filed with us to members
of the public. However, campaign statements are not filed with
the Commission. (See Section 84215.)

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh

_________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California
Administrative Code Section 18000, et seq. All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California
Administrative Code.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

TO:     Fair Political Practices Commission
        P.O. Box 807
        Sacramento, California

RE:     CAUSA

        1.  Enclosed is a copy of a Los Angeles Times article
dated January 12, 1984 stated that CAUSA donated $5,000.
to the Republican Party.

Question: Was CAUSA required to file with the FPPC or
the Registrar-Recorders office in Los Angeles to report
the $5,000 donation?

If so, was such a report filed with the FPPC.

If such a report was filed, please advise cost of a copy
of the filing.

        2.  CAUSA in 1984 also paid for air fare and hotel bills
for city councilmen and their wives for a trip to
Washington D.C.        The amount of money expended
by CAUSA exceeded $1,000.The councilmen did not report the gifts.

Question:   Was CAUSA required to file with the FPPC for
the gifts of air fare and hotel bills.

CAUSA is headquartered in Washington D.C.    CAUSA is a
part of Rev. Moon's Unification Church which has branches
in California.


Walter E. White
11124 Excelsior Dr., #21
Norwalk, California 90650

GOP Worker
Fired Over
Moonie Gift

By BARRY M. HORSTMAN,
Times Staff Writer

Los Angeles Republican Party
leaders, incensed to learn that a
recent statewide GOP youth conference was financed in part by an
organization affiliated with the Rev.
Sun Myung Moon's Unification
Church of America, Wednesday
fired the party worker who accepted the group's donation.

Jackie Clair, a fund raiser for the
party, said Los Angeles GOP leaders
demanded her resignation in the
aftermath of a report by The Times
that detailed how Clair had solicited
a $5,000 contribution from the Confederation of Societies of the Americas
(CAUSA) to help finance the GOP
youth conference. The organization,
an anti-communist group, receives
much of its funding from the Unification Church.

Called Irresponsible

"They told me I've blackened the
Republican Party's reputation all
across the state," said Clair, who
is treasurer of the California
Republican Youth Council, the
group that sponsored the Dec. 29-
Jan. 1 conference at Lake Arrowhead. "I'm being called the 'Waterloo of 1984.'"

Judy Ridgeway, executive director
of the Los Angeles County Republican Party, termed Clair's decision to
accept the $5,000 donation from the
church-related group a "totally irresponsible act that showed very
poor judgment on her part."

Ridgeway said the decision to fire Clair
made by the party's seven-
member board of directors.

The Republican Party of Los
Angeles County does not approve of
Rev. Moon's activities . . . and
certainly would not knowingly accept
a contribution from him,"
Ridgeway said. "Miss Clair never

[illegible] concerning [illegible]
for financial [illegible]
[illegible] for financial assistance [illegible]
God [illegible] the Clair [illegible]
[illegible] the assembly [illegible]
[illegible] said [illegible]
[illegible] about 1,500 [illegible]

Last Minute Effort to Clarify
His Park, assistant chair of the
convention conflicted stated to Party
Executive Director Ridgeway said
the Board of Equalization A
